name: Mike Longo
abstract: Michael Josef Longo (March 19, 1937 – March 22, 2020) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and author.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Mike Longo on piano... It was a great band, and Gil Fuller had called me and said, 'Jimmy, will you put together a big band for this tour?'
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: Mike was in the piano — was playing piano when I joined the group so we had a quintet at the beginning.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Ignacio Berroa) never mentions Mike Longo. Mike Longo is only mentioned by the interviewer as the source of an anecdote about Dizzy Gillespie. about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: you’re familiar with Mike Longo — he just passed away. He’s the one that has been preserving Dizzy’s rhythmic legacy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022
